response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
As a retired man from Chicago, I strongly believe that the best way to manage and deal with fake content on social media is by being cautious and critical of the information we consume. Here are some tips that have worked for me: 
Firstly, be skeptical of sensational headlines or posts that seem too good (or bad) to be true. Check the source of the information and verify its accuracy before sharing or believing it. 
Secondly, don't blindly follow or trust any one person or source on social media. Diversify your feed with different perspectives and sources to get a more well-rounded view of the world. 
Thirdly, use fact-checking websites or tools to verify the accuracy of information before sharing it. This can help prevent the spread of misinformation and fake news. 
Lastly, be aware of your own biases and try to approach information with an open mind. Don't let confirmation bias cloud your judgment and make you more susceptible to fake content. By following these tips, I have been able to effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media without compromising my critical thinking skills or ethical standards.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: My opinion is based on my personal experience and critical thinking skills, which I have honed through my years of life experiences, education, and exposure to different perspectives. I have found that being cautious and critical of the information we consume is essential in managing and dealing with fake content on social media.
